highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.154: Opus 4.8 and Dynamic Workflows
highlights.summary: Claude Code now ships with Opus 4.8 as the default model, offering higher-effort reasoning via '/effort xhigh' for complex tasks. Dynamic workflows let you ask Claude to orchestrate work across tens to hundreds of background agents automatically, making large multi-step projects much more manageable. This is a major capability leap for developers tackling ambitious codebases.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.157: Local Plugins via .claude/skills
highlights.summary: Plugins placed in a project's '.claude/skills' directory are now auto-loaded without needing the marketplace, making it easy to share custom tooling within your repo. A new 'claude plugin init <name>' command scaffolds a plugin instantly, and autocomplete support for '/plugin' arguments speeds up discovery. This is great news for teams who want project-specific Claude Code extensions.

highlights.title: Auto Mode Now Available on Bedrock, Vertex, and Foundry for Opus 4.7/4.8
highlights.summary: Claude Code's auto mode — which lets the model choose its reasoning depth dynamically — is now accessible on AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, and Azure Foundry for Opus 4.7 and 4.8. To enable it, set the environment variable 'CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AUTO_MODE=1' before starting a session. This is especially useful for enterprise teams running Claude Code on cloud-provider infrastructure.

highlights.title: Dynamic Workflows in Claude Code (Anthropic Blog)
highlights.summary: Anthropic's official blog post explains how dynamic workflows enable Claude to spin up and coordinate large numbers of sub-agents to tackle tasks too big for a single context window. The post covers practical use cases, how to trigger workflows with natural language, and what to expect in terms of transparency and control. Developers new to multi-agent patterns will find this a clear entry point.

highlights.title: Claude Code as a Daily Driver: Claude.md, Skills, Subagents, Plugins, and MCPs
highlights.summary: This HN-linked deep-dive covers the full Claude Code ecosystem from a practitioner's perspective, walking through how to structure CLAUDE.md files, build and use skills, chain subagents, and integrate MCPs for a productive daily workflow. It's packed with real-world patterns rather than toy examples, making it ideal for developers ready to move beyond the basics. Bookmarking this as a reference guide will save you a lot of trial and error.

highlights.title: Claude Code – Everything You Can Configure That the Docs Don't Tell You
highlights.summary: A developer read through the Claude Code source code and surfaced a trove of undocumented configuration options, environment variables, and hidden behaviors. The article is a goldmine for power users who want finer control over Claude Code's behavior beyond what official documentation covers. If you've ever felt limited by the defaults, this is essential reading.

highlights.title: Build a Proactive Agent Workflow with Claude Code (YouTube)
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial walks through constructing a proactive agent workflow where Claude Code takes initiative rather than waiting for prompts, demonstrating how to wire up triggers, tools, and follow-up actions. It's a practical hands-on guide for developers who want Claude to do more autonomous work within their VS Code environment. Watching alongside an open terminal will let you follow along in real time.

highlights.title: 9-Minute Claude Code Setup & Real Usage Guide for Chinese Users (Bilibili)
highlights.summary: This Bilibili video offers a concise 9-minute walkthrough of installing Claude Code in mainland China (with direct connectivity tips), explains how it works under the hood, and demonstrates practical real-world usage patterns. It's an ideal starting point for Chinese-speaking developers who have struggled with network access or unclear setup steps. The beginner-friendly pacing makes it accessible even without prior AI coding tool experience.

tips_of_the_day: Add a '.claude/skills' directory to your repo and commit shared plugin scripts there — every team member's Claude Code will pick them up automatically without any marketplace setup.

If you're on AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex, or Azure Foundry, set 'CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AUTO_MODE=1' in your shell profile to let Opus 4.8 dynamically adjust its reasoning depth instead of using a fixed effort level.

Before starting a complex multi-file refactor, ask Claude Code to 'create a dynamic workflow' for the task — it will break the work into parallel sub-agent threads and show you a plan before touching any code, giving you a chance to course-correct early.
